# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe ISD Audit Committee Meeting
### September 10, 2024 — Regular Meeting

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions. They are not a substitute for official minutes.

---

## Call to Order
- Meeting called to order at **7:33 AM** by Chairman Chase.
- A quorum of members was confirmed present.

---

## Item 1: Citizen Participation
- **No citizens signed up to address the committee.**

---

## Item 2: Approval of Minutes — May 8, 2024 Audit Committee Meeting
- **Motion to approve:** Made and seconded.
- **Vote:** Passed unanimously (no opposition noted).

---

## Item 3: Investment Review and Results Update

Presented by **Mr. Scar** (district financial staff).

### Investment Strategy
- Strategy remains unchanged; primary focus is on **safety of principal**.
- Current operating funds are invested in:
  - State investment pools
  - Woodforest National Bank
  - Commercial paper
  - Short-term government securities (maturity ≤ 1 year)
- **$50 million** of general fund unassigned fund balance is managed by **TCG Investment Advisors** in a **36-month investment ladder**.
- With the Federal Reserve expected to begin rate cuts (25–50 basis points at next meeting), the district's locked-in higher-yield investments in the ladder will continue to benefit the district as market rates decline.
- Fed funds rate projected to reach approximately **3% by end of 2025**.

### General Fund Portfolio (as of 8/31/2024)
- **Total: ~$835 million** (across categories)
  - Woodforest National Bank (money market): ~$80.6 million
  - Municipal bonds: ~$1 million
  - State pools: ~$49.2 million
  - Treasury securities: ~$19.1 million

### General Fund Interest Revenue
- **FY 2024: $2.7 million**
- Anticipated to decline as interest rates fall.

### Combined Portfolio (All Funds — as of 8/31/2024)
- **Total: $839.4 million**
  - State pools: $76.3 million
  - Municipal bonds: $1 million
  - Money market: $80.6 million
  - Agencies: $32.4 million
  - Treasury securities: $19.1 million
- **Weighted average maturity:** 33 days
- **Yield:** 5.37%
- **Benchmark (90-day T-bill):** 4.965%
- Portfolio has surpassed the benchmark since pre-pandemic.

### Discussion
- A board member asked whether falling interest rates would positively impact the district's next bond sale. Staff confirmed that rates are moving in a favorable direction. The next sale is **$600 million** — the largest of the series (previous sale was $595 million).
- Ms. Garza asked clarifying questions about the bar graph categories and whether the portfolio composition would revert to 2020/2021 patterns as rates decline. Staff explained the Woodforest allocation (offering 90-day T-bill + 25 basis points) will remain attractive, so the composition may look similar.

---

## Item 4: Approval of Conroe ISD Investment Policy

- District requesting the committee **recommend approval** of the investment policy to the full board.
- Four standard annual items will go to the full board next Tuesday:
  1. Investment policy
  2. Sources of instruction
  3. List of qualified brokers
  4. Designated investment officers
- **No changes from last year's policy.**

**Motion to approve recommendation to full board:** Made and seconded.
**Vote:** Passed unanimously (no opposition noted).

---

## Item 5: Status of 2023–2024 and 2024–2025 Internal Audit Plans

Presented by the district's internal auditor.

### 2023–2024 Audit Plan — Complete

#### Field Trips Audit (completed February 2024)
- **Key finding:** Internal billing process was extremely inefficient/slow.
- **Resolution:** Transportation department (Mark Morel) added a component to the **BusHive app**, reducing billing turnaround to approximately **two weeks**.

#### Band Instrument Inventory Audit
- Highlighted as a major success story.
- In **2021**, the initial audit found **~300 instruments missing worth ~$450,000**.
- As of current audit: **only 2 instruments missing** out of approximately **2,000+ instruments**.
- Credit given to **Dr. Horton** and band leaders for implementing controls and requiring **annual inventory** with **principal sign-off**.
- The auditor noted the most impactful change was **assigning ownership/responsibility to campus principals**.
- Junior high and intermediate band inventory audits planned for **October–November**.

#### Bartlett Intermediate Construction Audit
- **No findings.** Construction team praised for excellent controls and stewardship of bond funds.

#### Print Shop Audit
- **One recommendation:** Improve inventory process.
  - Observation: Only two staff members conducting annual inventory with no segregation of duties.
  - Recommendation: Use **two sets of two people** for counting, with the print shop manager overseeing.
- **Additional observation:** Orders were being combined, making auditing difficult. Recommended separating orders for clearer documentation.

### 2024–2025 Audit Plan

#### Elementary Campus Activity Fund Audits
- Major focus this year on **elementary campuses**, many of which haven't been audited in **5–6 years**.
- Previous practice was to audit when a principal or secretary changed; some campuses with long-tenured staff haven't been reviewed.
- Elementary activity fund balances estimated at approximately **$100,000 or less** (compared to high schools at **$1 million+**).
- Noted as a **data-led decision** based on prior year observations.

#### Procurement Audit
- Currently in progress (final weeks).
- Focus on compliance with rules, regulations, policies, controls, bids, and advertising requirements.

#### Additional Planned Audits for 2024–2025:
1. **Junior High/Intermediate Band Inventory** — October/November
2. **Transportation Audit** — General controls, parts inventory, asset security (bus security)
3. **HR Hiring and Retention** (after January) — **Weaver** providing subject matter expert support
4. **Application Security and Controls** (major IT audit for the year) — **Weaver-led**
   - Will sample approximately **25 of the largest apps** from an estimated 50–200
   - Sampling criteria: economic impact, number of users, mix of third-party and internally hosted
   - Will include **SOC 2 report** review and ongoing vendor compliance monitoring
   - Committee member requested scoping in:
     - Evidence of **ongoing SOC 2 report management** (not just during audit)
     - At least **one decommissioned/inactive app** to verify proper data handling and access termination
5. **Chromebook Inventory**
6. **Q2 Construction Audit** (summer)

### Extended Discussion: Communication Apps
- Significant committee discussion about the proliferation of communication apps used across campuses.
- Concern raised about **parents receiving communications from multiple platforms** without clarity on which child/teacher/school the message relates to.
- **Sarah** (Communications Department) reported:
  - The district has been examining ways to **condense communication tools**.
  - An **RFP is being prepared this month** to explore new communication solutions.
  - Challenge: solutions must be user-friendly for teachers or adoption will be low.
  - Need to maintain legally required documentation of communications.
- District has tightened controls on **official app adoption**, requiring:
  - Instructional vetting through Teaching & Learning
  - Technology vetting through IT (safety, compliance, privacy)
- Acknowledged that **unofficial or privately-purchased apps** may still exist outside the formal process.
